frameworks.name: Bayesian framework
frameworks.one_line: Posterior over fixation locations conditioned on global scene context

frameworks.name: saliency model (bottom-up)
frameworks.one_line: Local-feature saliency pathway taken alone is insufficient for search

frameworks.name: global scene gist
frameworks.one_line: Scene-centered holistic features bias attention before object analysis

core_question: How can global scene context be formally combined with local saliency to predict human fixations during real-world object search?
key_assumptions: Attention reflects a probabilistic estimate of target location
Global scene features can be computed in parallel with local saliency
Top-down task knowledge interacts with scene context at an early stage
manipulations: Pathway composition: saliency-only vs scene-context vs combined model
Natural search tasks across varied real-world scene images
key_findings: A two-pathway model combining local saliency and global scene features predicts fixated regions
Scene context constrains likely target locations beyond what saliency alone explains
interpretations: Contextual guidance arises early in visual processing through parallel global-feature computation
Object search is best modeled as Bayesian integration of saliency, context, and top-down goals
